segments.text: Jesus answered and spoke to them again in parables, saying,
The Kingdom of Heaven is like a certain king, who made a wedding feast for his son,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and sent out his servants to call those who were invited to the wedding feast, but they would not come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Again he sent out other servants, saying, ‘Tell those who are invited, “Behold, I have prepared my dinner. My cattle and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready. Come to the wedding feast!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his own farm, another to his merchandise;
and the rest grabbed his servants, treated them shamefully, and killed them.
When the king heard that, he was angry, and sent his armies, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.
Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding is ready, but those who were invited weren’t worthy.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Go therefore to the intersections of the highways, and as many as you may find, invite to the wedding feast.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those servants went out into the highways and gathered together as many as they found, both bad and good. The wedding was filled with guests.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “But when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man who didn’t have on wedding clothing,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you come in here not wearing wedding clothing?’ He was speechless.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the king said to the servants, ‘Bind him hand and foot, take him away, and throw him into the outer darkness. That is where the weeping and grinding of teeth will be.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For many are called, but few chosen.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the Pharisees went and took counsel how they might entrap him in his talk.
They sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying,
Teacher, we know that you are honest, and teach the way of God in truth, no matter whom you teach; for you aren’t partial to anyone.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Tell us therefore, what do you think? Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said,
Why do you test me, you hypocrites?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Show me the tax money.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They brought to him a denarius.
He asked them,
Whose is this image and inscription?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They said to him,
Caesar’s.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then he said to them,
Give therefore to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When they heard it, they marveled, and left him and went away.
On that day Sadducees (those who say that there is no resurrection) came to him. They asked him,
saying,
Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies, having no children, his brother shall marry his wife and raise up offspring[^67] for his brother.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now there were with us seven brothers. The first married and died, and having no offspring left his wife to his brother.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the same way, the second also, and the third, to the seventh.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: After them all, the woman died.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the resurrection therefore, whose wife will she be of the seven? For they all had her.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But Jesus answered them,
You are mistaken, not knowing the Scriptures, nor the power of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like God’s angels in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But concerning the resurrection of the dead, haven’t you read that which was spoken to you by God, saying,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the multitudes heard it, they were astonished at his teaching.
But the Pharisees, when they heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, gathered themselves together.
One of them, a lawyer, asked him a question, testing him.
Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the law?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
‘You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This is the first and great commandment.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A second likewise is this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The whole law and the prophets depend on these two commandments.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now while the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them a question,
saying,
What do you think of the Christ? Whose son is he?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They said to him,
Of David.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He said to them,
How then does David in the Spirit call him Lord, saying,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
‘The Lord said to my Lord, sit on my right hand, 
until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet’?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “If then David calls him Lord, how is he his son?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No one was able to answer him a word, neither did any man dare ask him any more questions from that day forward.
